fidelity.reason: The inventory allocation is attributed to an outside observer who does not control it.
evidence_spans: an outside observer who does not control the decision allocates a fixed stock

managerial_framing.reason: The allocation trade-off is concrete, but the responsible actor is not.
evidence_spans: Concentration pools scarce stock, while broader coverage protects more zones

scholarly_positioning.reason: It states a bounded pre-positioning question without a prior-knowledge comparison.
evidence_spans: when pre-positioning coverage is worth the loss of inventory concentration

evidence_discipline.reason: It limits the claim to the supplied one-period distributions.

prose_clarity.reason: The prose clearly expresses the allocation tension.
evidence_spans: Pre-positioning determines which realized orders can be served

overall_reason: The allocation actor fails the responsible-actor gate.
